questions.q: In which Indian city is the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah located?
questions.explanation: The Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah stands in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the east bank of the Yamuna river.

questions.q: By what affectionate nickname is the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah popularly known?
questions.explanation: Its white-marble beauty and resemblance to the later Taj Mahal earned it the nickname 'Baby Taj'.

questions.q: Which empire's architecture does the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah belong to?
questions.explanation: The tomb is a Mughal monument, built during the reign of Emperor Jahangir.

questions.q: Who commissioned the construction of the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah?
questions.explanation: Empress Nur Jahan, wife of Jahangir, built the tomb in memory of her father.

questions.q: For whom did Nur Jahan build the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah?
questions.explanation: Nur Jahan built the tomb for her father, the Persian-born nobleman who served as Jahangir's chief minister.

questions.q: What was the primary facing material that makes the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah so distinctive?
questions.explanation: It was the first Mughal monument faced entirely in white marble, a departure from the red sandstone used earlier.

questions.q: On the bank of which river does the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah stand?
questions.explanation: The tomb sits on the eastern bank of the Yamuna river in Agra.

questions.q: The decorative technique of inlaying colored stones into marble, seen at this tomb, is known as what?
questions.explanation: The tomb showcases pietra dura, called parchin kari, in which semi-precious stones are inlaid into marble.

questions.q: Which later, more famous monument in Agra is the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah often seen as a precursor to?
questions.explanation: Its marble inlay and design are widely regarded as a forerunner to the Taj Mahal, hence the 'Baby Taj' name.

questions.q: What type of structure is the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah?
questions.explanation: It is a mausoleum, built to house the grave of Nur Jahan's father and his wife.

questions.q: What was the real name of the nobleman whose title 'Itimad-ud-Daulah' the tomb commemorates?
questions.explanation: Mirza Ghiyas Beg, Nur Jahan's father, was given the title Itimad-ud-Daulah, meaning 'Pillar of the State'.

questions.q: During which Mughal emperor's reign was the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah built?
questions.explanation: The tomb was constructed during the reign of Jahangir, Nur Jahan's husband.

questions.q: In approximately which decade was the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah completed?
questions.explanation: The tomb was built between about 1622 and 1628, that is, in the 1620s.

questions.q: The title 'Itimad-ud-Daulah' translates roughly to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Itimad-ud-Daulah means 'Pillar (or Reliance) of the State', a high honor for the empire's chief treasurer.

questions.q: What distinctive feature crowns the four corners of the tomb building?
questions.explanation: Four octagonal towers resembling stubby minarets rise at the corners of the marble tomb.

questions.q: Rather than a large central dome, the roof of the tomb is topped by what kind of structure?
options: A low rooftop pavilion (barahdari) with a curved canopy roof
questions.explanation: Instead of a high dome, the tomb is crowned by a small square rooftop pavilion (barahdari) with a curved, vaulted canopy roof, unlike the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Mirza Ghiyas Beg, for whom the tomb was built, was the grandfather of which famous Mughal figure?
questions.explanation: Mumtaz Mahal, for whom the Taj Mahal was built, was the granddaughter of Mirza Ghiyas Beg through his son Asaf Khan, linking this tomb to the Taj Mahal's story.

questions.q: The Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah is set within what kind of garden layout?
questions.explanation: The tomb stands at the center of a charbagh, the classic four-quartered Mughal garden divided by water channels.

questions.q: Which design innovation seen here strongly influenced the later Taj Mahal?
options: Extensive pietra dura inlay on white marble
questions.explanation: The lavish pietra dura inlay on white marble pioneered here became a hallmark of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: What was Mirza Ghiyas Beg's high office under Emperor Jahangir?
questions.explanation: Mirza Ghiyas Beg served as Jahangir's chief minister and treasurer, earning the title Itimad-ud-Daulah.

questions.q: In addition to her father, who else is buried alongside him in the tomb?
options: His wife (Nur Jahan's mother), Asmat Begum
questions.explanation: The cenotaph of his wife Asmat Begum, Nur Jahan's mother, lies beside that of Mirza Ghiyas Beg.

questions.q: What is the rough footprint shape of the main tomb building?
options: A roughly square plan with corner towers
questions.explanation: The marble tomb has a roughly square plan with octagonal towers rising from its four corners.

questions.q: From which country did Mirza Ghiyas Beg's family originally migrate to the Mughal court?
questions.explanation: Mirza Ghiyas Beg was a Persian nobleman who migrated from Iran and rose to prominence at the Mughal court.

questions.q: Which delicate stone-carving technique, allowing light to filter through marble, is prominently used in the tomb's screens?
questions.explanation: Finely carved marble jalis (perforated lattice screens) filter light into the interior chambers.

questions.q: The tomb's marble inlay features stylized motifs of which of the following?
options: Wine vessels, cypress trees, and flowers
questions.explanation: Its inlay and painted decoration depict Persian-inspired motifs such as wine flasks, cypress trees, and flowers.

questions.q: Why is the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah considered a turning point in Mughal architecture?
options: It introduced the move from red sandstone to white marble with inlay
It was the first to use concrete
It was the first single-story Mughal building
questions.explanation: It marked the transition from red-sandstone construction to marble clad with pietra dura inlay, setting the stage for the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: On which bank of the Yamuna does the tomb lie relative to the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort?
options: The same (west) bank as the Taj Mahal
The opposite (east) bank from the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort
On an island in the river
Several kilometers inland from the river
questions.explanation: The tomb stands on the east (left) bank of the Yamuna, opposite the side where the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort rise.

questions.q: Which feature of the tomb's exterior most clearly reflects Persian influence brought by Nur Jahan's family?
options: Glazed and polychrome geometric inlay patterns
questions.explanation: The intricate geometric and floral inlay reflects strong Persian (Iranian) aesthetic traditions favored by Nur Jahan's family.

questions.q: Which statement about the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah's UNESCO status is accurate?
options: It is itself a standalone World Heritage Site
It is not individually inscribed, unlike the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort which are World Heritage Sites
It shares a single listing with the Red Fort in Delhi
questions.explanation: While Agra's Taj Mahal and Agra Fort are UNESCO World Heritage Sites, the Tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah is not individually inscribed.

questions.q: Nur Jahan, who built the tomb, held what unusual position in the Mughal empire of her time?
options: She was an exceptionally powerful empress who effectively wielded imperial authority
questions.explanation: Nur Jahan was one of the most powerful women in Mughal history, wielding extensive political influence during Jahangir's reign.
